hero.title: <1>Frontend</1> developer in training, building clear and well-structured interfaces.
hero.subtitle: I work with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS while building my path toward full stack. I focus on creating clean, responsive, and accessible experiences, with attention to detail and a desire to contribute to real projects.

about.title: Technical foundation, visual judgment, and a drive to build better.
about.description: I'm a Computer Engineering student at CUCEI and a frontend developer in training. My background comes from complete academic projects — management systems, databases, and data structures built from scratch. Today I'm focused on React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, with a goal of moving toward full stack. I care about writing clean, responsive, and maintainable interfaces, understanding both the user experience and code structure. I'm in an active growth phase: I don't overstate my experience, but I approach every project as an opportunity to practice, document decisions, and contribute responsibly.

photo.ariaLabel: Portrait of Juan Antonio Aguirre Mares
photo.alt: Profile photo of Juan Antonio Aguirre Mares

skills.title: Tools I use, foundations that support my learning.
skills.description: My profile combines programming fundamentals with modern frontend practice. I distinguish what I've already applied in projects from what I'm still reinforcing, to show clear and honest progress.

projects.title: Projects where I turn learning into functional interfaces.
projects.description: A selection of personal and academic work showing how I solve concrete needs, organize components, consume data, and document what I've learned in each process.

petcare.description: Web app for small and medium veterinary clinics, designed to organize patients, appointments, and clinical history with a scalable frontend foundation.
petcare.learned: Feature-based organization, reusable components with Tailwind CSS v4, and planning an offline-first PWA experience.

quicknotes.description: Notes app with search, filters, sidebar, and local persistence, focused on a simple experience for capturing and finding information quickly.
quicknotes.learned: State management in React, UI interaction modeling, and data persistence with localStorage.

framelix.description: Movie catalog SPA that consumes the TMDB API, allows browsing film details, and organizes navigation with React Router.
framelix.learned: REST API consumption, route handling, Git collaboration, and deploying a frontend app on Netlify.

vetcare.description: Desktop system for veterinary management: appointments, clients, patients, medical history, vaccination, and inventory in a centralized workflow.
vetcare.learned: Relational data modeling, desktop interface design, and coordinating teamwork with Git.

technest.description: Management system for a tech store, built in C++ with OOP to handle inventory, suppliers, and orders.
technest.learned: OOP application, separation of concerns, and basic management system design.

dataStructures.description: Collection of data structures and algorithms implemented from scratch to reinforce logic, data traversal, and algorithmic reasoning.
dataStructures.learned: Practical understanding of efficiency, data organization, and problem-solving from first principles.

roadmap.title: A growth path with concrete steps.
roadmap.description: My current route prioritizes strengthening frontend, adding backend with intention, and building complete projects where each new technology has a practical and measurable application.

01.title: Frontend Fundamentals
01.description: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript applied to responsive layout, semantics, and language basics.

02.title: React + TypeScript + Tailwind
02.description: Components, state, static typing, and building consistent interfaces.

03.title: REST API Consumption
03.description: Fetch, async data, handling loading and error states.

04.title: Node.js
04.description: First steps with server-side JavaScript and backend structure.

05.title: Express & custom APIs
05.description: Building endpoints, routes, validations, and server logic.

06.title: Databases
06.description: Reinforcing SQL, relational modeling, and persistence with PostgreSQL.

07.title: Full Stack Project
07.description: An end-to-end application integrating frontend, backend, data, and deployment.

education.title: Technical training applied to real projects.
education.description: My learning combines the academic foundation of Computer Engineering with consistent practice in frontend development. I aim for every course, project, and technology to translate into better decisions around code, structure, and interface.

university.title: Computer Engineering
university.description: University degree focused on programming, data structures, databases, OOP, and foundations for building software with logic and organization.

frontend.title: Modern frontend in practice
frontend.description: Project-driven learning guided by official documentation and iterative improvement of clean, responsive, and maintainable interfaces.

contact.title: Have an idea in mind?
contact.description: I'm open to internships, frontend projects, and new opportunities where I can contribute, learn, and keep growing. Whether you have a proposal, an idea, or just want to say hello — I'd love to hear from you.

toast.error: Could not send the message. Please try again.
